name: M5 - State Storage System
objective: Implement modularshoot:states registry, per-gun/per-player/per-bullet three-tier state storage, GunState/PlayerState view APIs, per-gun sync with throttling, per-player AttachmentType sync, per-bullet state in BulletSnapshot, tooltip state bar, and resolveGunFromSnapshot utility

reference_files.reason: System 11: State Storage System - complete specification for three-tier storage, registry, view APIs, sync, tooltip integration
M5 milestone scope and verification criteria
API overview - modularshoot:states registry, ModularShootAPI utility methods, DataPackRegistry mechanism

exit_criteria: StateDefinition record + Codec completed with domain/value_type/default_value/display fields
modularshoot:states registry API (Java + data pack JSON) implemented, filling M1 stub
7 value types (int/long/double/float/boolean/string/uuid) supported with Codec dispatch
per-gun state storage (GunData.state Map) with NBT persistence implemented
per-player state storage (NeoForge AttachmentType with .sync()) implemented
GunState view API with type-safe get/set for all 7 types + hasState/clearState implemented
PlayerState view API with same signature as GunState implemented
per-gun state sync via GunSyncS2CPacket with 2-tick throttling strategy implemented
per-player AttachmentType sync configured with IAttachmentSerializer
Unregistered/type mismatch handling returns zero value + WARN (per-state per-minute rate limited)
resolveGunFromSnapshot utility method implemented (gunInstanceUuid -> shooter -> inventory search)
tooltip state bar integration with display.priority sorting, hide_default filtering, per-player mix
Project compiles successfully (gradle build / compileJava no errors)
Verification: kill count state increments on lethal hit and syncs to client tooltip; per-player state persists across weapon switches
